name: Wined Fish Chunks in Broth
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Good quality dry white wine
recipeInstructions:
Marinating: Have a fishmonger clean the fish and discard the head and fins. Wash the fish, then cut across into 1-1½ inch sections. Mix salt and white wine in a bowl. Add fish chunks; rub with marinade; cover bowl; refrigerate for at least 6 hours. After 2 hours, turn the fish to mix with the marinade.
Preparations: Wash cabbage and slice leaves down the middle, then into 2-inch sections. Rinse beancurd and cut into 1-inch cubes. Wash, trim, and shred scallions (greens and all). Peel and mince ginger. Heat salad oil until it smokes; remove from heat and reserve.
Cooking: Rinse fish chunks and drain. Heat chicken stock, beancurd, ginger, and salt in a saucepan. Reduce heat, cover pan, and simmer for 10 minutes. When ready to add fish and cabbage, increase the heat to boil; add fish and cabbage when liquid boils; cover pan. Fish and cabbage are cooked in about 3-5 minutes – cabbage leaves will be bright lime green.
Ladle fish, cabbage, and beancurd into a warm shallow serving bowl; add soup. Garnish with cooked oil, minced scallion, and pepper. Serve.
